heading: Promoting transparency and accountability in the Department of State workforce
label: 2734g Promoting transparency and accountability in the Department of State workforce
notes: Rule of Construction, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. I, title XCIV, § 9405136 Stat. 3897Dec. 23, 2022“Nothing in this title [enacting this section] may be construed as altering existing law regarding merit system principles.”

DefinitionsFor definitions of “Secretary”, “Department”, and “appropriate congressional committees” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 9002 of Pub. L. 117–263section 2651 of this title
amendments: Rule of Construction, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. I, title XCIV, § 9405136 Stat. 3897Dec. 23, 2022“Nothing in this title [enacting this section] may be construed as altering existing law regarding merit system principles.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary should establish a mechanism to ensure that appointments or details of Department career employees to staff positions in the Office of the Secretary, the Office of the Deputy Secretary of State, the Office of the Counselor of the Department, any office of the Secretary’s Policy Planning Staff, and any office of an Under Secretary of State, and details to the National Security Council, are transparent, competitive, inclusive, and merit-based.

children.heading: Report
children.label: b Report
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall submit a report to the appropriate congressional committees regarding the mechanism established pursuant to subsection (a).December 23, 2022
use transparent, competitive, inclusive, and merit-based processes for appointments and details to the staff positions specified in subsection (a); and
ensure that such positions are equally available to all employees of the Civil Service and the Foreign Service of the Department.
